name: Ring courtyard (O) silhouette
description: Overall silhouette whose footprint is a closed ring (O) around a fully enclosed central courtyard — all four sides built, the court open only to the sky. The archetypal cloister, sì-hé-yuàn, or peristyle domus: rooms face inward onto the protected court, which is the social and light-giving heart, while the outer wall is a continuous defensible façade. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides the ring.
placement_rules: Footprint is a closed rectangular ring around a central open courtyard.
Courtyard occupies 25-45% of the bounding box; ring thickness >= 3 (so >=4-wide rooms fit with an inner wall).
Rooms open inward onto the courtyard with windows or an arcade.
The courtyard is open to the sky (no roof over it).
An entrance passage pierces one side of the ring into the court.

renaissance.notes: Peristyle court with a ground-floor loggia on all four inner faces.

chinese.notes: Sì-hé-yuàn ring with lacquer-red walls and upturned eaves around a paved court.
